buggy_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
plt.plot(months, revenue)
plt.show()
task: Plot monthly revenue over time and label the axes and title so the chart is self-explanatory
error_location: line 2-3: plt.plot / plt.show — no xlabel, ylabel, or title calls
clarification_query: What labels and title should be added to make the chart self-explanatory?
clarification_options: No labels are required — the data speaks for itself
corrected_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
plt.plot(months, revenue)
plt.xlabel('Month')
plt.ylabel('Revenue')
plt.title('Monthly Revenue')
plt.show()
explanation: The original code creates the line plot but omits axis labels and a title. Adding plt.xlabel, plt.ylabel, and plt.title makes the chart self-explanatory.
